data.title: As a plugin maintainer, I can run review-stats.sh and see basic review statistics
data.description:
## Description

**What**: A reporting CLI script (review-stats.sh) that reads JSONL events, compacts fragments, and outputs a summary table with basic review metrics, plus a thin /dso:review-stats skill wrapper
**Why**: This is the user-facing payoff — the first time a maintainer can see aggregate review data. Combined with Story 2, this completes the walking skeleton.
**Scope**:
- IN: Read all .jsonl files, compact per-agent fragments into date-partitioned files, output pass/fail rates by review type, average dimension scores, finding severity distribution, average revision cycles, commit duration and failure rate. Default 30-day window with --since and --all flags. Thin skill wrapper.
- OUT: Compound heuristic for review-caught bugs, tier/overlay analytics (Story 6)

## Done Definitions

- When this story is complete, review-stats.sh reads JSONL events, compacts fragments into date-partitioned files, and outputs a summary table with basic metrics
  <- Satisfies: 'review-stats.sh CLI command reads JSONL events and outputs a summary table'
- When this story is complete, the script defaults to a 30-day window with --since and --all flags
  <- Satisfies: 'review-stats.sh defaults to a 30-day window'
- When this story is complete, review-stats.sh tolerates concurrent writes by skipping malformed JSONL lines with a warning rather than failing
  <- Satisfies: 'Concurrent event writes' (reader safety)
- When this story is complete, a thin /dso:review-stats skill wraps the CLI command
  <- Satisfies: 'A thin /dso:review-stats skill wraps the CLI command'
- When this story is complete, unit tests verify aggregation logic with fixture JSONL data
  <- Satisfies: unit test DoD

## Considerations

- [Performance] Compaction + time-windowing mitigate read cost; initial implementation must handle fragmented files
- [Reliability] Reader must tolerate partial/malformed lines from concurrent writes — skip with warning, not abort
